findings.finding: Launcher writes additional swarm-state.json fields and enum values that are absent from the commander agent schema.
evidence: bin/stampede.sh can set phase=commander_launch_failed and status=blocked/failed.
agents/stampede-commander.agent.md only documents phase claiming|decomposing|launching_squad_leads|launching_workers|collecting|complete and status running|success|partial|failed.
findings.impact: Strict validators based on the documented schema would reject launcher-created or blocked/failed commander states.

findings.finding: launch_proof has a clear required block, but automated validation is incomplete.
evidence: agents/stampede-commander.agent.md requires squad_leads_started, workers_started, first_child_started_at, last_child_started_at.
tests/smoke-test.sh validates launch_proof_required and some telemetry/depth/model fields, but not the nested launch_proof fields.
bin/stampede-monitor.sh warns from telemetry/launch_proof but does not enforce schema completeness.
findings.impact: A commander may pass smoke coverage while omitting or corrupting launch proof metadata.

findings.finding: Telemetry counters structurally exist, but success/partial correctness depends on counts that are not validated against child-agents.jsonl by tests.
evidence: Commander contract requires full success only when squad_leads_launched == 50 and workers_launched == 250.
No automated test was found that recomputes launched/completed/failed counts from child-agents.jsonl and compares to swarm-state.json.
For run-20260430-180646 commander-005, worker reported counters at zero with only commander_registered evidence at time of review, indicating incomplete launch proof for that artifact state.
findings.impact: Counters can drift from ledger evidence or indicate stalls without failing validation.

findings.finding: The documented phase enum implies claiming -> decomposing -> launching_squad_leads -> launching_workers -> collecting -> complete, but runtime uses extra initial/error/blocked phases.
evidence: bin/stampede.sh writes commander_pane_starting before the commander agent takes over.
bin/stampede.sh writes commander_launch_failed or arbitrary blocked reason phases such as auth_blocked.
bin/stampede-monitor.sh only exempts commander_pane_starting and claiming from no-proof warnings.
findings.impact: Terminal failure/blocked states may produce misleading monitor warnings and schema failures.

findings.finding: Allowed status values differ across swarm-state.json, bundle/result JSON, state.json, monitor, and merge logic.
evidence: swarm-state schema documents running|success|partial|failed, while launcher writes starting and blocked.
Commander bundle statuses are success|partial|failed, worker results use done/error, synthesizer also accepts completed, and observed artifacts include complete.
stampede-merge.sh only merges status == done, so commander-style success bundles are not part of that merge path.
findings.impact: Status ambiguity can cause skipped results, invalid validation outcomes, and inconsistent synthesis behavior.

findings.finding: Documented commander bundle schema includes telemetry but not the full nested launch_proof block, despite launch proof being central to metaswarm verification.
evidence: agents/stampede-commander.agent.md bundle shape lists telemetry counts but no nested launch_proof object.
Commander docs require writing bundle.json atomically, but worker review found historical commander-005 artifact dirs lacking bundle.json.
findings.impact: Final bundles may be insufficient as standalone launch-proof artifacts.

recommendations: Create one explicit JSON schema for swarm-state.json covering launcher initial, running, blocked, failed, partial, and success states.
Either add runtime phase/status values to the documented enum or map launcher-only states to documented values plus a separate reason field.
Add smoke/schema tests for all top-level required fields, all launch_proof nested fields, and telemetry counters derived from child-agents.jsonl.
Require bundle.json to include launch_proof or a verifiable reference to swarm-state.json plus child-agents.jsonl ledger evidence.
Normalize result and bundle status enums, and update merge/synthesis logic to handle commander success|partial|failed intentionally.
